learning_outcomes.description: Explain the fundamental anatomy and physiology of various domestic and exotic animal species.

Describe the role of pets in society and be able to identify the common breeds of domestic and exotic animal species, and recognise the major topical issues around pet ownership, including preventative health programs, animal behaviour and husbandry, and practical competencies from a safety perspective relevant to industry engagement.
Describe wildlife management practices and develop an understanding of wildlife professional responsibilities and ethical considerations
Review and critically appraise peer-reviewed veterinary literature.

assessments.due_date:
Comparative Anatomy Quiz 29/07/2026 - 28/08/2026

Dog Behaviour & Handling Quiz 26/08/2026 - 30/10/2026

Cat Behaviour & Handling Quiz 26/08/2026 - 30/10/2026

Birds & Exotics Behaviour & Handling Quiz 26/08/2026 - 30/10/2026

Bovine Handling and Restraint Quiz 26/08/2026 - 30/10/2026

Students are required to complete 5 quizzes arising from practical work. While each quiz can be completed after a student completes the related practical, it is a requirement that all quizzes be completed by the due date specified.
assessments.task_description:
There will be a small quiz at the end of each of FIVE (5) practical classes with the quizzes relating to the content covered during each practical. Each Quiz has 5% weighting. The quizzes will be available via the ANIM1120 Blackboard site. The students need to complete the respective quiz exam after attending the practical.

Further information will be available on the ANIM1120 Blackboard site.
assessments.submission_guidelines: All quizzes are due at 5:00PM on the due date.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.assessment_title: Applied animal behaviour assignment and presentation
assessments.task_description: This assessment requires students to review and critique a selected journal article that reports on a behaviour intervention technique and will include preparation of a 5min oral presentation (recorded). Guidelines outlining the requirements and structure of the report will be provided during the tutorial (starting week 5) and also made available on Blackboard under Assessment.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description: This exam will examine all lecture content taught during Semester 2.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

learning_activities.topic:
Comparative anatomy practicals and Animal behaviour, handling and restraint practicals involving cattle, dogs, cats, avian and exotics

Five scheduled practical classes will be conducted in groups throughout the semester. During the first practical (Weeks 1–4), students will develop their understanding of comparative anatomy through hands-on study of a range of animal species, including ruminants, dogs, cats, birds, reptiles, and other small mammals.

From Weeks 5–13, four species-specific handling and restraint practicals will be conducted in parallel. Students will rotate through practical sessions involving cattle, dogs, cats, avian and exotics, gaining experience in safe and appropriate handling and restraint techniques. Each student will be scheduled to attend each practical session once during the semester, ensuring exposure to all species groups.


Sub-activity: Group wise - Comparative anatomy of dogs, cats and avian and exotic animal species - Week 1 - 4 Group wise - Animal handling - Week 5 -13

learning_activities.topic:
Lectures

Lecture series on companion animal and exotic pet behavior, handling and management

Sub-activity: Comparative anatomy of ruminants, dogs, cats and avian and exotic animal species - Week 1 Companion animal handling & restraint - Week 2 to 3 Avian and exotics animal species and their husbandry - Week 3 to 8 Dog sports & service dogs - Week 9 Companion animal nutrition, husbandry, housing & enrichment - Week 10 to 11 Industry placements lecture - Week 11
